item.title: 🇨🇳 China Signals Readiness to Export J-35A Fighter Jet to Pakistan
item.description: State media showed a J-35A model with AVIC logo and English lettering, suggesting China's first export of its new stealth fighter may go to Pakistan amid growing defense ties.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US Intelligence: Iran No Closer to Nuclear Weapon Despite War
item.description: US intelligence agencies estimate Tehran remains about a year away from being able to build a nuclear weapon, despite weeks of fighting, suggesting the conflict has not significantly degraded Iran's nuclear timeline.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Attacks Kill 15 Ukrainian Civilians in Past Day
item.description: Russia launched 11 Iskander ballistic missiles and 164 Shahed drones in overnight attacks, killing 15 civilians and injuring at least 72 across Ukraine despite the declared ceasefire.

item.title: 🇫🇮 Finland Joins Ukraine-Led Corpus Defense Procurement Coalition
item.description: Finland has joined the Ukraine-led Corpus defense procurement coalition, aiming to strengthen defense procurement coordination and supply chain resilience among partner nations.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Hails Leap Forward in Ties With Russia's Ally Armenia
item.description: The EU and Armenia laid the groundwork for closer ties at a summit in Yerevan, marking another step in the former Soviet republic's pivot away from Moscow toward the West.

item.title: ⚔️ US Army Fires Tomahawk from New Typhon Launcher in Philippines Drill
item.description: The US Army successfully fired a Tomahawk cruise missile from its new Typhon launcher during joint exercises with the Philippines, showcasing the system's capabilities in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thailand Scraps 25-Year Oil Deal with Cambodia
item.description: Thailand cancelled a decades-old joint petroleum exploration agreement with Cambodia in the Gulf of Thailand amid territorial disputes and changing regional dynamics.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Urges Restraint, Support for US-Iran Mediation
item.description: Saudi Foreign Ministry called for restoring maritime navigation in the Strait of Hormuz to its pre-war state, supporting diplomatic efforts to de-escalate tensions between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 💥 Russia Claims It Shot Down Nearly 300 Ukrainian Drones
item.description: Russia's Defense Ministry said it intercepted 298 Ukrainian drones overnight in one of the largest drone attacks of the war, as Kyiv continues strikes deep into Russian territory.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Announces Sanctions Targeting Russia's Fighter Recruitment and Drone Production
item.description: The UK unveiled new sanctions aimed at disrupting Russia's military capabilities, specifically targeting recruitment networks for fighter pilots and drone manufacturing facilities.

item.title: ⚡ Sharif Calls for Ceasefire to Be Respected After Iran Attacks on UAE
item.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister urged all parties to honor the ceasefire following Iran's missile strikes on the UAE, highlighting regional concerns about escalation.

item.title: 💰 IMF Chief Warns of 'Much Worse' Outcome If Middle East War Drags Into 2027
item.description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva warned that prolonged conflict in the Middle East could severely impact global economic stability, with oil prices and supply chains at risk.

item.title: 🛢️ UAE-Saudi Oil Talks Spotlight as OPEC Navigates War Disruption
item.description: UAE and Saudi Arabia are coordinating on oil policy as the Iran conflict threatens Gulf energy exports, with OPEC weighing production adjustments amid market volatility.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden Arrests Chinese Captain Suspected of Russia-Linked Vessel
item.description: Swedish authorities arrested a Chinese national captain suspected of involvement in a Russia-linked vessel, amid investigations into sabotage and infrastructure threats in the Baltic Sea.

item.title: 🏛️ Germany's Merz Marks Year in Office Facing Deep Transatlantic Crisis
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz marks his first year in office confronting strained relations with Washington and challenges to European security architecture.

item.title: 🇷🇴 Romanian Government Survives No-Confidence Vote as Pro-EU Coalition Holds
item.description: Romania's coalition government survived a no-confidence motion brought by opposition parties, maintaining its pro-European Union course amid political turbulence.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia Hosts Summit with Russia and EU as Yerevan Seeks Balance
item.description: Armenia hosted a trilateral summit with Russia and the EU, as Yerevan attempts to maintain relations with Moscow while deepening ties with Brussels amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇸🇩 Sudan War: Ethiopia Accuses RSF of Drone Attacks, UAE Role Questioned
item.description: Ethiopia accused Sudan's RSF paramilitary of launching drone attacks from UAE-supported bases, adding regional dimensions to the Sudanese conflict.

item.title: ⚔️ Russia-Ukraine Truce Tested as Both Sides Report Violations
item.description: Both Russia and Ukraine accused each other of violating a temporary ceasefire, with drone and missile attacks continuing despite declared truce intentions.

item.title: 🏛️ Defense Contractor Leidos Raises 2026 Forecast on Strong Demand
item.description: Defense contractor Leidos raised its 2026 revenue forecast citing strong demand from government contracts amid increased military spending globally.

item.title: 📰 Reuters Wins Pulitzer for Meta Investigations
item.description: Reuters won the Pulitzer Prize for beat reporting for its investigative series examining Meta's operations and their impact on society.

item.title: 🔫 US Secret Service Shoots Gunman Near White House
item.description: US Secret Service shot an armed individual near the White House, with the suspect taken into custody after the confrontation.

item.title: 🌍 Strait of Hormuz Control Tested as US Guides Ships Through Iranian Fire
item.description: US naval forces escorted commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian fire, testing Tehran's ability to control the critical waterway amid ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: ⛽ Russian Attack Hits Ukraine's Naftogaz Gas Facilities
item.description: A Russian strike killed five people and damaged Naftogaz gas production facilities in Ukraine, intensifying pressure on Kyiv's energy infrastructure.

item.title: ⛪ BBC: Pope Leo XIV Elected as First American Pontiff
item.description: Cardinals elected Pope Leo XIV as the first American pope in history, marking a significant shift for the Catholic Church.
